title: Day 08: Diamond Armor Gym Shine, Broken Promises & Paranoia Detox
mood: high confidence, diamond sweat, developer guilt, demographic shock, fast-breaking simpery
heroLine: Sweating literal diamonds in Minecraft armor while coding promises burn. Paranoia at the gym? Time to pull out the Simp card.

nowPlaying.label: Obsessed with (Shining bright like a diamond)

body: Great day, guys. As I said in my previous blog, I am feeling a bit confident in myself. I initially wanted to keep Rihanna's 'Diamonds' for my picture, but I found it a bit too feminine, while I wanted my picture to look masculine—so I almost went with Fred again..'s 'Victory Lap Five' instead. But honestly, I really wanted to keep Rihanna's song, so Rihanna it is! Regardless, the whole point is the sweat on my face and body: the gym lights reflecting off those sweat droplets make them look like literal diamonds. I looked like I was covered fully in Minecraft diamond armor. Look at this shiny, diamond-plated warrior in the Reebok tee who still can't lift 35kg properly. Peak performance.

But let's pivot to my stellar integrity. I previously gave a solemn promise to Mustang that I would learn web development and build a website for her. Flash forward to today: that promise has been officially filed into the garbage bin. Every single day, my schedule for coding is: plan to start, feel guilty, open a sitcom, and do absolutely nothing. The procrastination loop is undefeated. We need to stop this procrastination at any cost. Let's see what will happen for tomorrow, but if history is any indicator, I'll probably just write another blog entry complaining about my laziness.

Now, let's talk about my sudden, irrational gym paranoia. Today, I realized that almost all of the gym members present were Muslim, and the gym itself is also owned by a Muslim. I only noticed this today because all the friends that I know were not there in the gym—not even one person I know! It instantly made me feel somewhat weird. I'm not saying I hate this, but I am scared a little bit. I don't know why, but I need to work on this. I guess I don't hate anyone, but I am scared of them a little bit. In the future, if anyone seeing this blog wants to call me Islamophobic, please keep your opinions to yourself because my first true love is a Muslim girl! For fuck's sake, I have done fasting for Ramzan with her—such a lame person I am. So yeah, I don't hate anyone, I'm just a guy who gets a bit startled by his own shadow in a room full of strangers.

scraps.title: Diamond Armor Selfie
scraps.text: Sweat droplets under the gym lights looking like diamonds. Kept the Rihanna song because the shine is too real.

image.alt: Mirror selfie of Yaswanth in Reebok t-shirt sweating at the gym

scraps.title: Web Dev Promise Garbage
scraps.text: Promised Mustang a website, got a lifetime subscription to procrastination instead.

image.alt: Laptop displaying HTML code next to crumpled papers

scraps.title: The Simp Credentials
scraps.text: For those calling me names: I did the full Ramzan fasting for my first love. S-level simping registered.
